title: Next.js Editor
content:
Next.js Code Editor

Interactive Next.js Playground

Practice Next.js code directly in your browser! This editor simulates a full Next.js development environment where you can write App Router pages, Server Components, API routes, and more.


What You Can Practice

  • App Router - /app directory structure
  • Server Components - Data fetching on server
  • Client Components - 'use client' directive
  • API Routes - /api handlers
  • Layouts & Pages - Nested layouts
  • Dynamic Routes - [slug] params

Pro Tips

  • Use Ctrl + Enter (or Cmd + Enter on Mac) to quickly run your code
  • Your code is automatically saved
  • Editor simulates full Next.js 14+ with App Router

title: Next.js Introduction
content:
Next.js Introduction

The Full-stack React Framework

Next.js is a React framework for production. It provides features like server-side rendering (SSR), static site generation (SSG), API routes, image optimization, and file-based routing out of the box.

Why Next.js?

  • Performance: Automatic code splitting, image optimization
  • SEO: Server-side rendering
  • Developer Experience: File-based routing, layouts
  • Full-stack: API routes + frontend

title: Next.js Installation
content:
Next.js Installation

Create Next.js App

Use the official CLI to scaffold a new Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind, and App Router.

Terminal Commands
npx create-next-app@latest my-next-app --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias "@/*"
cd my-next-app
npm run dev

Check Versions
npx next --version
npm list next

title: App Router Basics
content:
App Router (Next.js 13+)

The New Folder-Based Routing

App Router uses the /app directory for routing. Every folder is a route segment.

Folder Structure
app/
  layout.tsx     ← Root layout (wraps all pages)
  page.tsx       ← Homepage (/)
  about/
    page.tsx     ← /about
  dashboard/
    page.tsx     ← /dashboard
    layout.tsx   ← Dashboard layout

title: Server Components
content:
Server Components

Default Server-Side Rendering

Server Components run only on the server. No client JavaScript bundle needed. Perfect for data fetching.

Server Component (Default)
// app/page.tsx
import { fetchData } from '@/lib/api';

export default async function HomePage() {
  const data = await fetchData(); // Runs on server
  
  return (
    

      
{data.title}

      
    {data.items.map(item => 
  • {item.name}
    • )}

    

  );
}

title: Client Components
content:
Client Components

Client Component
'use client';

import { useState } from 'react';

export default function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
  
  return (
    

      
Count: {count}

      
    

  );
}

use client Directive

Add at top of file to enable React hooks and browser APIs.

title: Data Fetching
content:
Data Fetching Patterns

Server-Side Data Fetching

fetch() in Server Components
// app/users/page.tsx
async function getUsers() {
  const res = await fetch('https://api.example.com/users');
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed');
  return res.json();
}

export default async function UsersPage() {
  const users = await getUsers();
  return (
    
    
      {users.map(user => (
        
  • {user.name}
    • 
      ))}
    

  );
}

title: Layouts
content:
Layouts & Templates

Root Layout (app/layout.tsx)
import './globals.css';

export const metadata = {
  title: 'My App',
  description: 'Next.js App',
};

export default function RootLayout({
  children,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  return (
    
      
        
Navigation

        
{children}

        
Footer

      
    
  );
}

Layouts Persist Across Routes

title: Dynamic Routes
content:
Dynamic Routes & Params

app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx
import { notFound } from 'next/navigation';

type Props = {
  params: { slug: string };
};

export default function BlogPost({ params }: Props) {
  let post;
  
  switch (params.slug) {
    case 'first':
      post = { title: 'First Post', content: '...' };
      break;
    default:
      notFound();
  }
  
  return (
    

      
{post.title}

      
{post.content}

    

  );
}

title: API Routes
content:
API Routes

app/api/users/route.ts
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export async function GET() {
  const users = [{ id: 1, name: 'John' }];
  return NextResponse.json(users);
}

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const body = await request.json();
  // Save to DB
  return NextResponse.json({ message: 'Created', data: body });
}

Available at /api/users

Supports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE methods.

title: Deployment
content:
Deployment to Vercel

One-Click Deploy

Vercel CLI
npm i -g vercel
vercel login
vercel --prod
Environment Variables
# .env.local
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://...
NEXTAUTH_SECRET=...
NEXTAUTH_URL=https://myapp.vercel.app

title: Performance
content:
Rendering Strategies

Choose Your Strategy

  • SSG: Static at build time
  • SSR: Render on each request
  • ISR: Static + revalidate
ISR Example
export const revalidate = 3600; // Revalidate every hour

export default async function Page() {
  const data = await fetchData();
  return 
{data.content}
;
}

title: Next.js Best Practices
content:
Next.js Best Practices

Folder Structure

Recommended
src/
  app/           # App Router
  components/    # UI components
  lib/           # Utilities, DB
  types/         # TypeScript types
  hooks/

Rules

  • Co-locate components with pages
  • Keep Server Components as default
  • Use parallel routes for dashboards

title: Next.js Summary
content:
Next.js Course Summary

You Did It!

Congratulations on completing Next.js!

  • App Router: Modern routing system
  • Server Components: Zero-client-JS data fetching
  • Layouts: Shared UI across routes
  • API Routes: Full-stack capabilities
  • Deployment: Vercel optimized

Next Steps

  • Build a full-stack blog
  • Integrate with database (Prisma)
  • Learn authentication (NextAuth)
  • Explore advanced patterns

Keep building with Next.js!
